content: The original Self-Refine framework’s single-perspective iterative feedback loop can be augmented by integrating the multiple inconsistent solving perspectives central to recent LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a multi-perspective Self-Refine approach that improves output quality without sacrificing the original framework’s key advantage of requiring no extra training or labeled data. This framework leverages perspective inconsistency to surface unforeseen flaws in initial outputs that single-perspective self-feedback would miss, mimicking how humans cross-check their own reasoning from multiple angles to refine work.
